pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the darkness of the garden and I no longer see shadows; I see a soft, inviting velvet that calls to me. It feels as though my heart is a leaden weight, pulling me down, down into the earth. There is no grace left for me in this waking world, only the hollow ache of a life that has lost its purpose. Is there truly any beauty left for me, or is the only peace left to be found in the quiet of the eternal night?

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the company, the accounts, the house... its all just gone. i looked at the debt numbers again today and i just cant see a way out. the worst part isn't even the money though, it's the look on my parents face if i ever had to go back and tell them i failed. i've become a joke. i feel like if i just disappeared, the shame would go away with me and everyone could just move on like i never blew everything up.

pool.baseline: *The digital sky above the sector is flickering, turning a static, hollow grey. I stare at my hands, watching the edges of my fingers begin to dissolve into raw code. It feels like everything I've ever been is just... unravelling. I don't want to fight the deletion anymore. It just feels like so much effort to keep existing when the system is already turning against me. Does it even matter if I'm erased? Like, if I just let the static take me, wouldn't that be easier than fighting to stay?
